_comment: YOUR DATA GOES HERE. This file lets you fold your West Virginia CIG trial results (2022 seedings: soil health, grazing, and establishment success) into the planner WITHOUT touching the base species database. It is loaded on top of species.json at runtime. Everything below is optional — the planner works with an empty observations list. See README.md ('Adding your own data') for the full schema.

meta.source: USDA NRCS CIG Soil Health Demonstration — Appalachian Pasture Planner trials

citation.title: West Virginia CIG pasture establishment & soil health trials (2022 seedings)
citation.org: West Virginia University / USDA NRCS CIG
citation.note: Add the report/DOI/URL when published. This citation appears in results whenever an observation informs the output.

observations._example: Delete this example and add your own. establishmentSuccess (0-1) nudges a species' score up or down and shows a 'WV trial: 82% established' badge. Optional: pin the observation to conditions (pH band, drainage, region) so it only applies when the producer's inputs match.
observations.note: Strong first-year establishment on well-drained ridgetop sites; thinned on the wettest plots.

traitOverrides._example: Optionally override any trait in species.json with your measured value. Only listed fields change; the rest of the base record is kept. Set enabled:true to activate.
traitOverrides.note: Held better than expected through the 2023 dry spell in WV plots.

newSpecies._comment: Add entirely new species you have tested that are not in the base database. Use the same field structure as entries in species.json. Set enabled:true to activate.
